name: Amy Wolkowinsky
school: School of Informatics, Computing, and Cyber Systems
focus: Research coordination • Interdisciplinary collaboration • Program support
bio: Amy J. Wolkowinsky is an earth scientist and Arizona-licensed geologist with expertise at the intersection of climate science, data-driven decision-making, and applied research. As Associate Director of the Ecosystem Science & Innovation Lab (ECOSAIL) at NAU and Affiliate Faculty in the School of Earth and Sustainability, she focuses on connecting researchers, practitioners, and partners to advance applied, solutions-oriented work. Her background spans environmental consulting, academia, and climate tech, with experience supporting grant development, cross-sector collaboration, and innovation-driven projects. At the Radiant Center, Amy aims to help bridge disciplines and perspectives, strengthening the connection between emerging science and real-world applications across diverse environmental systems.
